Program Objectives

Rapidly evolving industrialization and technology increases the usage of electricity with every passing day – so much so that there is no area, where electricity is not used. In this sense, teaching qualified technical staff in electricity carries
    great importance with respect to developing technologies and increasing the added value for the economy.

The Program of Electricity aims to educate ELECTRICAL TECHNICIANS, who are well-equipped to follow science and technology, have analytical thinking skills, can use, interpret and analyze their knowledge, respond to the expectations of the business world
    and the industry and have the working potential on national and international levels.

Furthermore, students get to me

Moreover, students will be required to complete an internship of a total of 30 business days, where they will reinforce their theoretical knowledge with practice and seize the opportunity to meet their future workplaces and coworkers, while they are still
    students.

Graduates of this program can assume the title of electrical technician and work at various institutions and companies, carry out all affairs and transactions regarding electrical work, solve electrical issues and ensure the distribution and transmission
    of electricity. They can work at many governmental institutions as technicians, while having the chance to find a job in the private sector or opening their own businesses. Additionally, they can find jobs in technological infrastructure companies,
    research development institutions, technological consultancy and application companies, production companies, service companies, lighting industry, industrial control and automation companies.

Career Opportunities

They can work at many governmental institutions as technicians or find a job in the private sector. In addition to public and private sectors, they can start their own businesses.

They can work at technological infrastructure companies, research development institutions, technological consultancy and application companies, production companies, service companies, lighting industry, industrial control and automation companies.

Institutions that work in the energy sector (private sector companies and TRT or Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources etc.)

Technical services of electrical home appliances

Electrical project offices

Construction firms

Plants that manufacture electrical machinery

Firms that work with renewable energy resources (wind, solar, geothermal, biomass, gases from biomass (including methane), wave and current energy)

Electrical wiring and lighting system companies

Firms that distribute electricity

Technical departments for constructional work within universities

Technical operation departments of large scale hotels

R&D institutions that manufacture and provide maintenance for aviation, satellite and marine telecommunications systems (TÜBİTAK, ROKETSAN, TAI, ASELSAN and so on)

Automotive industry

Machinery manufacturing industry

Chemistry sector

Firms that build hydroelectricity facilities

Workplaces that provide industrial maintenance and repairing services
